settings.custom_liquid: ⚡ Direct from tannery✓ Pit-tanned 20+ yrs🚚 Ships same day TX

settings.question: What is the difference between 1st and 2nd grade?
settings.answer: 1st grade hides are the cleanest cuts with minimal natural markings — ideal for tooled show saddles or presentation pieces. 2nd grade hides have more natural character (brands, scars) but the same structural strength, making them perfect for working saddles at a better value.

settings.question: What thickness should I use for saddle skirts?
settings.answer: For saddle skirts we recommend 13–14oz (Extra Heavy) or 14oz+ skirting leather. The extra weight gives your skirts the rigidity and longevity that working saddles demand. For lighter applications like saddlebags or tooled accessories, 9–10oz is a great choice.

settings.question: How long does shipping take?
settings.answer: We ship same day from Greenville, Texas for orders placed before 3PM Mon–Fri. Most US orders arrive in 1–3 business days. You'll receive a tracking number by email as soon as your order ships.

settings.question: Can I call before ordering to ask about a specific hide?
settings.answer: Absolutely. Manuel is available Mon–Fri 8AM–3PM CT at (972) 750-2810. He can walk you through current inventory, help pick the right grade and thickness, and answer any technical questions.

settings.question: What if the leather isn't what I expected?
settings.answer: Contact us — we'll make it right. We stand behind every hide we ship. Reach Manuel at manuel@theskirtingfactoryllc.com or (972) 750-2810 and we'll work out a solution together.
